name: Hispanic Chocolate Three-Chile Mole
recipeIngredient: 4 Corn tortillas (6-inch diameter), cut into half-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover all the chilies and soak for 30 minutes. Reserve 3 tablespoons (80 ml) of the soaking water.
2. Stem and seed the chilies. In a medium skillet, heat 3 tablespoons (80 ml) of lard over medium heat.
3. Fry the chilies for 30 seconds on each side. Remove the chilies from the skillet and add the sesame seeds; stir until toasted lightly, about 1 minute. Remove the sesame seeds from the skillet and set aside.
4. Add 3 tablespoons (80 ml) of lard to the skillet and, when hot, add the tortilla strips. Fry until soft, about 2 minutes.
5. Place the chilies, sesame seeds, tortilla strips, and reserved soaking water in a blender and puree to a paste; set aside.
6. In a large skillet, heat the remaining lard over medium-high heat. Sauté the onions until translucent, about 3 minutes.
7. Add the tomatoes and cilantro and cook for 2 minutes. Add the bay leaf, cinnamon, pepper, salt, cloves, cumin, cayenne, and oregano and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes, or until well blended.
8. Add the chopped chocolate and continue cooking and stirring until the chocolate is melted, about 2 minutes.
9. Add the chicken stock and cashews and bring to a boil. Add the pureed chili mixture and stir well. Lower the heat and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 15-20 minutes, or until the mixture thickens and the flavors have blended.
10. Serve with grilled pork tenderloin, duck breasts, chicken, or turkey. Makes about 3 cups.
